skill.name: linear
skill.description: Manage Linear issues, projects, documents, and team workflows through a connected Linear MCP server.
skill.summary: Use Linear MCP tools to plan, triage, update, and report on team work.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command and network patterns, but most are documentation examples or Linear MCP tool names. The confirmed concern is that the skill guides users to configure a remote Linear MCP server and includes an optional WSL command that runs npx mcp-remote, which is legitimate for this integration but requires user trust and OAuth permissions.

medium_findings.title: Remote MCP Setup and Command Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to add a remote Linear MCP endpoint, enable the remote MCP client, authenticate with OAuth, and optionally run mcp-remote through npx on Windows. This is expected for a Linear integration, but it grants a remote service access to Linear data within the user's permissions and introduces supply-chain risk from the npx command.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines directly show setup commands for a remote MCP endpoint and npx mcp-remote. The behavior is legitimate for the skill purpose, so the concern is elevated operational risk rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Matches
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography on descriptive text and system reconnaissance on a troubleshooting tip. No cryptographic algorithm, host enumeration command, or reconnaissance workflow is present in the reviewed lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines contain prose such as description text and rate-limit advice, not security-sensitive commands or cryptographic code. This makes the static high-severity crypto matches highly likely to be false positives.

low_findings.title: Backtick Tool Names Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged many backtick-delimited Linear tool names as Ruby or shell execution. These entries are documentation labels for MCP tools, not executable shell syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines list tool names such as issue, project, document, and comment operations. They do not instruct the assistant to run a shell or interpolate user input into a command.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Remote MCP Invocation
dangerous_patterns.description: The Windows troubleshooting configuration runs npx with mcp-remote against the Linear MCP SSE endpoint. This pattern can execute package-managed code and should be reviewed before use in controlled environments.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is visible in the skill file and has a real execution path. It appears to support a legitimate Linear MCP workaround, so confidence is high for the pattern but not for malicious intent.

content.value_statement: Teams lose time when issue updates, planning, and documentation checks happen in separate tools. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a structured workflow for using Linear MCP safely and consistently.
actual_capabilities: Guides setup for the Linear MCP server and OAuth login.
Supports reading, creating, and updating Linear issues.
Organizes workflows for sprint planning, bug triage, and release planning.
Helps search Linear documents and create documentation follow-up issues.
Structures bulk updates with read-first and confirm-before-write steps.
Summarizes results, blockers, and recommended next actions.
limitations: Requires a connected Linear MCP server and valid workspace permissions.
Cannot access Linear data outside the user's authorized workspace scope.
Bulk changes can hit Linear rate limits or require smaller batches.
Remote MCP setup and OAuth should be reviewed in restricted environments.

use_cases.title: Plan a Sprint
use_cases.description: Review open issues, select priority work, and prepare a cycle with assignments and follow-up tasks.

use_cases.title: Triage Incoming Bugs
use_cases.description: List high-priority bugs, group them by impact, and update labels or statuses after confirmation.

use_cases.title: Audit Product Documentation
use_cases.description: Search Linear documents for gaps, then create clear issues for outdated or missing documentation.

prompt_templates.title: List My Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Show my open Linear issues for this week. Group them by status and flag anything blocked or overdue.

prompt_templates.title: Triage Bugs
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate workflow for support and engineering teams.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find critical and high-priority bug reports for the mobile team. Summarize impact, owner, and recommended next status.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a Release Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a release planning outline for project v2.0. Review open blockers, suggest milestones, and draft issues for missing launch tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Balance Team Workload
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with bulk review before updates.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review active issues for the platform team. Group work by assignee, identify overloaded owners, and propose reassignment changes before applying them.

output_examples.input: Review my active Linear issues and tell me what needs attention.
output: A status summary grouped by issue state.
A short list of blocked, stale, or overdue issues.
Suggested next actions for each priority item.

output_examples.input: Plan a bug triage pass for this sprint.
output: A ranked list of bugs by priority and user impact.
Recommended label and status changes for review.
A concise summary of gaps, owners, and follow-up comments.
A summary of relevant Linear documents found.
Draft issue descriptions for documentation fixes.

best_practices: Confirm team, project, issue IDs, and write permissions before making changes.
Read Linear data first, then present grouped updates before applying them.
Use smaller batches for large updates to reduce rate-limit and review risk.
anti_patterns: Do not apply bulk status or assignment changes without user confirmation.
Do not connect the Linear MCP server to untrusted workspaces or accounts.
Do not use vague issue creation requests without priority, owner, and acceptance details.

faq.question: Does this skill require Linear access?
faq.answer: Yes. It requires a configured Linear MCP server and an OAuth login with access to the target workspace.

faq.question: Can it create and update issues?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide MCP calls to create issues, update fields, add labels, and create comments.

faq.question: Can it plan full projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes workflows for release planning, milestones, dependencies, and project follow-up tasks.

faq.question: Does it work without a remote MCP server?
faq.answer: No. The skill depends on Linear MCP tools being connected and available to the AI environment.

faq.question: What data can it access?
faq.answer: It can access Linear data allowed by the authenticated user's workspace permissions.

faq.question: Is it safe for regulated teams?
faq.answer: It may be usable after review. Teams should approve the remote MCP endpoint, OAuth scope, and any npx usage first.
